title: P5.2 — Decide determinism tolerance and exception policy for C-09
description: Approve the determinism acceptance boundary and exception mechanism. This is the G2 owner disposition input; G4 implements the failing comparison and approved-exception check. (§5 C-09; §8 P5; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P5 — Operational contract owner decisions (§8)
description:
Owner-decision tier for performance/memory budgets, cache semantics, curl-removal closure, oracle policy, determinism tolerance, migration window, and graduation gates.

Stable contradiction consumers: C-08 and C-09; C-12 also consumes oracle policy. C-10 remains a P0 release-boundary decision, with P5 only supplying downstream operational criteria.

Create atomic decision leaves under the G0.3 schema; accepted tracker status alone is non-normative. (§8 P5; RCON-09–RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G0.3 — Record schemas + lifecycle state machine (§6 authority records + transition table)
description:
§6 artifact 2 + lifecycle. Parent task for G0.3a common artifact envelope/validator and G0.3b invalidation/re-open semantics.

Define tracked schemas for immutable owner decisions, accepted designs, and source-claim, decision, contradiction, and finding registers. Encode all ten states and allowed transitions with actor, timestamp, evidence, and dependency IDs.

Import/validate P0.1 without rewriting substance; enforce append-only accepted decisions, single writers, referential integrity, and non-authoritative tracker state.

Done only when both child validators pass and the schemas can drive later leaf slicing. (§4 RCON-01; §6; §7 G0)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P2.4 — Decide standards and scheme admission boundary for C-11
description: Decide how ADR 0012 supersedes ADR 0004, including where the older closed scheme set remains compatibility/default policy. This supplies the P2 admission portion of C-11; P4 supplies specializations. (§5 C-11; §8 P2; RCON-05)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P0.3 — Tracker topology, snapshot boundary, mutation and single-writer rules
description:
What: Decide final epic placement, tracker export/query and as-of boundary, allowed mutations during review, tracker-to-record ID rules, and who may write each mutable control artifact.

Done: an immutable accepted owner record exists; final topology is applied before snapshot freeze; tracker-only comments/status cannot become normative authority; reparenting and later mutations are auditable. (§4 RCON-01; §7 G0; §8 P0)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: C-05 — Dispose encoded-slash compatibility versus WHATWG serialization

title: G4.1 — Verify cache equivalence and documentation consistency (C-08)
description:
Implement executable positive/negative checks for the accepted cache contract: cached/uncached equivalence, bounds/eviction/clearing/version keys, and runtime-versus-documentation consistency.

This verifies the C-08 disposition; it must not choose cache semantics. (§5 C-08; §7 G4; RCON-09)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G1.2 — Build §6 artifact 4 public-surface inventory registry
description:
Build artifact 4's inventory phase: every export, argument, behavior dial, default, interaction, vector/error behavior, documentation location, migration surface, and test family receives an inventory disposition.

Missing/inaccessible evidence must be explicit. This issue constructs the registry at G1; a separate G3 issue closes it normatively against artifacts 3 and 5–10. (§6 artifact 4; §7 G1/G3; RCON-05)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: Epic: rurl 3.0 protocol hardening — amend & re-review before product-spec draft
description:
Scope: §11 next frontier + §7 pre-draft gates. Product-spec drafting remains out of scope until durable G5 acceptance.

Two interlocking structures:
- gate acceptance order G0→G1→G2→G3→G4→G5;
- owner-decision tiers P0→P5, consumed through atomic durable records rather than tracker status.

PHASING: schema-dependent leaf fan-out remains deferred until G0.3 and P0.3. Stable work is instantiated now: four atomic P0 decisions; P1–P5 containers; known contradiction decision leaves; C-01–C-12 dispositions; S1–S9 evidence transfer; artifact-wide validator/invalidation semantics; artifact-4 G1→G3 handoff; semantic cache closure; C-08/C-09/C-10 G4 verification; and dual G5 review.

DURABILITY: authoritative manifest/registers/decisions/findings and ten-state lifecycle are tracked, schema-validated records. Accepted records are append-only; tracker-only decisions have no authority. The manifest separates source baseline 89be90b from the later frozen control-plane snapshot and hashes a tracker export.

SEQUENCING: G2 resolves every contradiction before G3; G4 verifies already-approved policy. Any invalidated upstream record fails/reopens downstream acceptance.

Depends on RURL-jairsufw. Placement remains provisional until P0.3 records final topology before snapshot freeze. (§1, §4–§11; RCON-01–RCON-10; C-01–C-12)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

comments.author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com
comments.content: Independent roadmap review fixes applied: split P0 and removed C-10 from G0 prerequisites; instantiated P1–P5 and contradiction-specific owner decisions; added C-01–C-12 dispositions with corrected cross-tier wiring; added evidence transfer, universal artifact validation, invalidation/re-open semantics, artifact-4 handoff, semantic cache closure, G2 acceptance, G4 verification-only leaves, and durable G5 review fan-in. Remaining schema-shaped leaf expansion stays deferred until G0.3/P0.3.

title: P5.1 — Decide authoritative cache contract for C-08
description: Choose the authoritative cache default and semantic-transparency/documentation policy. This is the G2 owner disposition input; G3 closes the semantic contract and G4 implements the documentation-consistency gate. (§5 C-08; §8 P5; RCON-09)

description:

Decision scope: canonical tracked paths, schema format/versioning, raw-evidence archive exception, stable identities, checksums, and how source-baseline versus control-plane snapshot revisions are represented.

Done: an immutable accepted owner record exists under the P0.1 approval mechanism and names the affected G0 deliverables. (§4 RCON-01; §6 artifact 1; §8 P0)

title: P2.1 — Decide fixer/recovery ordering for C-02 and C-03

title: P1 — State and public object owner decisions (§8)
description:
Owner-decision tier for canonical state/public object, scalar/vector result behavior, authority/credential/path/query/delimiter/source-byte state, and compatibility projections.

Create atomic decision leaves after G0.3 fixes the record schema. Stable cross-tier consumers already identified: C-07 requires P1 plus P2; artifact 3 and public-surface cells consume this tier at G3.

Every accepted outcome must be a durable owner record; completing this tracker container alone conveys no authority. (§8 P1; RCON-02, RCON-04)

title: C-11 — Apply ADR 0012 supersession to scheme admission precisely

title: C-02 — Choose browser-fixer ordering and supersede the other claim

title: C-01 — Reconcile accepted wrappers with internal draft metadata
description: Produce a typed disposition correcting durable metadata or marking stale status fields superseded; classify mixed claims assertion-by-assertion until corrected. Record authority, rationale, affected claims, and invalidated artifacts. (§5 C-01; §7 G2; RCON-01, RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G0.3a — Common artifact envelope and validator for §6 artifacts 1–11
description:
Define the shared normative envelope required for every §6 artifact: schema/version, tracked location, owner/single writer, lifecycle state, dependencies, completion rule, input/output hashes, approval evidence, and exact validation command.

Implement validation for duplicate IDs, broken references, unknown states, missing owner/approval/completion fields, and manifest hash drift.

Done: artifacts 1–11 can be mechanically rejected when the universal §6 contract is incomplete. (§6; RCON-01–RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G1.1 — Transfer S1–S9 evidence with bidirectional coverage validation
description:
Transfer—not summarize from memory—the detailed S1–S9 findings, owner questions, fixture families, matrix skeletons, and implementation paths into durable records.

Each transfer records report identity/hash, stable finding ID and locator, target DEC/CON/REQ/FIX/matrix record, disposition state, and §10 canonical path correction where applicable.

Generate forward and inverse checks against §9 so every frozen finding is represented exactly once in the inverse grouping while allowing documented multi-RCON links. (§9–§10; RCON-01, RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: C-08 — Dispose cache runtime/documentation contradiction
description: At G2, choose the authoritative behavior and record the required correction. Do not wait for G4: G3 closes the cache semantics and G4 only implements/verifies the documentation-consistency gate. (§5 C-08; §7 G2–G4; RCON-09)

title: G0 — Durable control plane & restore (RCON-01)

title: G4 — Executable evidence (RCON-09/10, §6 artifact 11) [remaining leaves deferred]
description:
§7 G4 + §6 artifact 11. Depends on G3 contract closure.

G4 verifies accepted contracts; it does not resolve contradictions or choose product policy. Instantiated leaves:
- G4.1 cache equivalence and documentation consistency verifies C-08;
- G4.2 failing determinism comparison and approved exceptions verifies C-09;
- G4.3 release-rule verification verifies C-10.

Remaining schema-shaped leaves: fixture/oracle catalog; metamorphic properties; claim traceability; benchmark/memory budgets; curl zero-reference gate; full-string/state/vector/mutation/join/migration coverage; exact CI commands.

Artifact 11 receives the same §6 schema/owner/lifecycle/location/completion contract. (§6 artifact 11; §7 G4; RCON-09, RCON-10; C-08–C-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P2.3 — Decide validation verdicts and legacy status migration for C-07
description: Decide validation verdict/fatality/annotation boundaries and migration of legacy parse_status. This supplies the P2 portion of C-07 and must agree with P1.1. Produce an accepted durable owner record. (§5 C-07; §8 P2; RCON-04)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G5 — Independent re-review & acceptance (§7 G5)
description:
§7 G5. Depends on complete G4 evidence.

Instantiated leaves:
- context-free comprehensibility/completeness review;
- independent evidence-aware citation/executable-claim review;
- findings closure and final acceptance fan-in.

Both reviews bind exact protocol and manifest hashes. Every finding uses the durable lifecycle. Any material normative/source change invalidates both reviews and reopens final acceptance.

Only accepted G5.3 with objective yes/no evidence may unblock a successor product-spec draft. (§6 lifecycle; §7 G5; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: C-12 — Retire projection-based conformance claims and define rebuild boundary
description: Jointly apply P2 output and P5 oracle decisions. Preserve historical projections as evidence only and define the full-state/full-string basis for rebuilt claims. (§5 C-12; §7 G2; RCON-03, RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: C-04 — Separate standard serialization from legacy clean output
description: Apply the P2 output decision to ADR 0012 and shipped clean serializers; decide unimplemented versus superseded intent and preserve fragment policy explicitly. (§5 C-04; §7 G2; RCON-03)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: C-09 — Dispose evidence-only determinism workflow contradiction
description: At G2, approve determinism tolerance, failure policy, and exception authority. G4 later implements the failing comparison and exception validation. (§5 C-09; §7 G2–G4; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G5.3 — Review-finding closure and final protocol acceptance
description:
Fan-in for G5 acceptance.

Dispose every finding from both independent reviews in the durable finding register; verify closure state and objective yes/no evidence for every amended-protocol acceptance question; require the accepted protocol hash to equal the reviewed hash.

Any material normative change invalidates both reviews and reopens this acceptance. Only this accepted durable gate record may unblock a successor product-spec draft. (§6 lifecycle; §7 G5; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P0.4 — CRAN hold scope and exact v2/v3 release boundary (C-10)
description:
What: Dispose C-10 by naming the exact release line governed by the CRAN hold and reconciling it with the accepted v2.7 disposition.

Why: this is release authority, not a durability prerequisite.

Done: a typed, durable owner decision records authority, rationale, affected claims/releases, and invalidated artifacts. G2 consumes the disposition; G4 verifies the resulting release rule. (§5 C-10; §8 P0; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G1 — Baseline & inventory closure (§7 G1) [remaining leaves deferred]

§7 G1 + inventory obligations from RCON-05/RCON-09. Depends on the objective G0.4 restore drill.

- G1.2 builds artifact 4's public-surface inventory phase.

Remaining schema-shaped leaves are cut after G0.3/P0.3: curl inventory; accepted-claim, test-family, benchmark, migration, external-source and evidence-completeness inventories.

Exit: manifest pins both source and control-plane snapshots plus external inputs/checksums; every required surface has a disposition; missing evidence is explicit; all G1 children and validation commands pass. (§7 G1; §9–§10; RCON-01, RCON-05, RCON-09, RCON-10)

title: G5.1 — Context-free amended-protocol review

title: P1.1 — Decide v3 state/status model needed by C-07
description: Decide the canonical state/public projection and legacy parse_status projection needed to distinguish syntax, policy, and optional facts. This supplies the P1 portion of C-07; P2 owns verdict/fatality behavior. Done only when an accepted durable owner record exists. (§5 C-07; §8 P1; RCON-02, RCON-04)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P0 — Process & release authority (§8)
description:
§8 P0 container for four separately approved, durable owner decisions. The reconciliation answers none of them.

Children:
- P0.1 bootstrap owner identity + approval mechanism
- P0.2 durable workspace + manifest format
- P0.3 Fiberplane topology + snapshot/mutation/single-writer rules
- P0.4 CRAN hold scope + exact v2/v3 release boundary (C-10)

P0 is a tier/container, not one atomic decision. G0 consumes P0.1–P0.3 only. P0.4 is disposition input for C-10 at G2 and downstream verification at G4; it does not block durability work.

Authority rule (§4 RCON-01; §6): each accepted child decision must exist as a tracked immutable owner record. Tracker status is work state only.
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G2 — Authority & contradiction closure (§5)
description:
§7 G2 + §5 contradiction register. Starts after G1, but passes only through child G2.A.

C-01–C-12 are explicit disposition tasks with corrected owner routing. C-08/C-09/C-10 are policy-disposed here; G4 only implements/verifies their accepted outcomes. Cross-tier wiring is explicit for C-07, C-11, and C-12.

Exit: every known/new contradiction has a typed durable owner disposition; claim roles remain assertion-distinct; invalidation edges validate; no blocking OPEN/CONFLICT/INVALIDATED record remains; G2.A records input hashes and acceptance evidence. (§5; §6 lifecycle; §7 G2; C-01–C-12; RCON-01–RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G4.2 — Implement failing determinism comparison and approved exceptions (C-09)
description:
Implement the accepted P5/C-09 policy as a failing cross-platform comparison with an explicit approved-exception registry, claim boundaries, exact local/CI commands, and positive/negative fixtures.

This verifies rather than resolves C-09. (§5 C-09; §7 G4; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P3 — Mutation, identity and joins owner decisions (§8)

Owner-decision tier for mutation transactions/rollback, key equivalence/versioning/collisions, six-join semantics, and canonical_join migration.

Stable contradiction consumer: C-06. Contract artifacts 8–9 consume the remaining decisions at G3.

Create atomic decision leaves under the G0.3 schema; accepted tracker status alone is non-normative. (§8 P3; RCON-06–RCON-07)

title: G0.2 — Machine-readable manifest (§6 artifact 1)
description:
§6 artifact 1.

Create the machine-readable manifest in the P0.2 format, including distinct source_baseline_commit and control_plane_snapshot_commit/tag fields; source inventory; hashed tracker export/query/as-of boundary; artifact hashes; tool versions; external revisions; and tested restore instructions.

Give the manifest a schema, owner/single writer, lifecycle, completion rule, and tracked location. Done requires schema validation and referential integrity to owner decisions, findings, contradictions, and supersession records. (§4 RCON-01; §6 artifact 1; §7 G0–G1)

title: G5.2 — Evidence-aware citation and executable-claim review
description: An independent evidence-aware reviewer checks every material citation and executable claim against the frozen manifest/baseline, including exact commands and oracle boundaries. Record reviewer identity, reviewed hash, findings, and evidence. (§7 G5; RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: P0.1 — Bootstrap owner identity and durable approval mechanism
description:
What: Establish the minimal tracked bootstrap authority envelope needed before the general §6 record schema exists.

Why: P0 otherwise depends on G0.3 for the mechanism that makes P0 authoritative, recreating RCON-01.

Required record: exact decision text, owner/approver identity and authority, timestamp, source baseline, evidence, scope, supersession rule, and an explicit rule that tracker-only decisions have no authority.

Done: the bootstrap record is tracked and approved; G0.3 is required to import and validate it without silently rewriting its substance. (§4 RCON-01; §6 artifact 2/lifecycle; §8 P0)

C-12 also requires P5 oracle policy. Produce atomic durable decisions. (§5 C-04, C-05, C-12; §8 P2; RCON-03, RCON-10)

title: G2.A — Authority and contradiction gate acceptance
description:
Acceptance fan-in for §7 G2.

Pass only when C-01–C-12 plus every newly discovered contradiction have typed durable dispositions; accepted/shipped/historical/proposed/superseded claims remain assertion-distinct; dependency edges propagate source changes; and no blocking OPEN/CONFLICT/INVALIDATED record remains.

Record exact input hashes and validation results. Any later invalidation fails/reopens this acceptance and blocks G3 onward. (§5; §6 lifecycle; §7 G2; C-01–C-12; RCON-01, RCON-10)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G3 — Contract-matrix closure (§6 artifacts 3–10) [remaining leaves deferred]
description:
§7 G3 + §6 artifacts 3–10. Depends on durable G2.A acceptance, not merely the G2 header.

Deferred contract-family leaves remain: canonical state; standard/profile/scheme; validation/intervention; five outputs; cleaning/mutation; key/six joins; host/annotations; cross-artifact consistency.

Instantiated corrections:
- G3.4 explicitly closes artifact 4 after its G1 inventory phase;
- G3.9 closes semantic cache behavior from C-08 before G4 evidence.

Every artifact uses the common §6 envelope: schema, owner/single writer, lifecycle, tracked location, completion rule, hashes, and validator. Exit requires no unowned cells and cross-artifact agreement. (§6; §7 G3; RCON-02–RCON-09)

title: P4.1 — Decide scheme specialization posture needed by C-11
description: Decide specialization/compatibility treatment for the closed web scheme set versus arbitrary general schemes, including affected FTP-family and scheme-relative posture. This supplies the P4 portion of C-11. (§5 C-11; §8 P4; RCON-05, RCON-08)
author: 142225707+bart-turczynski@users.noreply.github.com

title: G0.3b — Gate invalidation, stale propagation and re-open semantics
description:
Define executable propagation from changed source/owner decisions to INVALIDATED dependent records, replacement DISCOVERED records, and failed/reopened downstream gate acceptance.

Tracker done state must not keep a gate accepted when its durable acceptance record is stale. Leaf preparation may overlap, but G0→G5 pass order remains strict.

Done: transition fixtures cover source delta, owner supersession, invalidated review, replacement record, and downstream gate failure/reapproval. (§6 lifecycle; §7 G0–G5; RCON-01, RCON-10)

When evidence is transferred, use §10 canonical tracked paths rather than stale report paths. Copying/freezing is not evidence transfer; the G1 transfer issue owns that work.

title: P4 — Scheme and host specialization owner decisions (§8)
description:
Owner-decision tier for credentials/mailto, FTP family, scheme-relative posture, host vocabulary, IDNA, PSL, DNS, and IP behavior.

Stable cross-tier contradiction consumer: C-11 requires both P2 admission and P4 specialization. Artifacts 5 and 10 consume the remaining decisions at G3.

Create atomic decision leaves under the G0.3 schema; accepted tracker status alone is non-normative. (§8 P4; RCON-05, RCON-08)
